# Luke Leigh — Personal Context for AI Post Writing

This file gives the AI enough personal background to write authentic opening anecdotes and
scene-setting for blog posts. It is **not** a brand spec — for brand voice rules, see
`.github/skills/brand-voice/SKILL.md`. This is about the actual person.

> **Maintained by Luke.** Keep it honest and specific — generic context is useless.
> The AI should use this to place you in a real situation, not invent a plausible sysadmin.

---

## Who Luke Is

- **Name:** Luke Leigh (online: BanterBoy)
- **Location:** Essex, UK
- **Role:** IT contractor — infrastructure engineering, PowerShell development, automation
- **Company:** Leigh Services / Leigh IT Services Limited (solo contractor)
- **Background:** Started in IT in August 1994 as a Computer Support Technician at Seetec in Hockley, Essex — maintaining customer networks on-site and remotely, building and configuring systems. No formal qualifications; entirely self-taught. Spent 6 years there before moving into telecoms support (Callserve Communications, Isle of Dogs — "Europe's largest internet telecommunications company" at the time), then into 2nd line and eventually Infrastructure Manager at IRIS Software (London, legal sector client base). Landed at Insure and Go Insurance Services for 8.5 years (2008–2016) as Network Architect, Webmaster, and PCI Compliance Specialist — the longest single engagement and the one that defined a lot of technical depth: WAN ownership, PCI DSS, multi-site infrastructure across Southend, Bristol, London and Wales, data centre migrations. Post-Insure and Go moved into shorter contracts — FSI Cloud (IaaS/private cloud), Six Degrees Group (MSP, 3rd line), Solopress (VMware/Cisco Meraki rebuild — where PSCiscoMeraki was written in personal development time), Ventrica (PCI compliance, twice), Carpetright (Senior Infrastructure Engineer). Set up Leigh IT Services Ltd in September 2022, running the contracting vehicle alongside the current engagement.
- **Current work context:** Infrastructure Engineer at Rail Delivery Group (London, September 2022 – present) — recruited to assist with a transition to new premises and general system management/maintenance. On-site in London. Running Leigh IT Services Ltd simultaneously as the contracting vehicle. Previous clients span insurance, retail, contact centres, cloud/MSP, and legal — mostly private sector, mix of on-site and remote. PCI DSS compliance is a recurring specialism across multiple engagements.

---

## Technical Background & Day-to-Day Tools

- **Primary tool:** PowerShell — daily, deeply, enthusiastically
- **Other tools/tech you work with regularly:** Azure, Active Directory, Hyper-V & VMWare (mostly because of the PowerShell), Windows (Every version since Windows 3.11 for Workgroups), GitHub Desktop/GitHub, VS Code, Agentic Development, AdminByRequest (privileged access management — used at client sites), LM Studio, Ollama, OpenCode (local LLM stack — actively running and experimenting with models locally)
- **Home lab:** Not so much a lab, more like a 25 User Office 365 Development Environment (Big Boys Toys) and the odd home server or two, incorporating Home Assistant and a number of Servarr tools. Unifi Networking with UDM. Also runs a Plex media server (hostname: `plexassistant`) and a self-hosted WORLDVIEW orbital tracking tool.
- **Editors/IDEs:** VS Code (primary)
- **Things you've been doing long enough to be mildly embarrassed about still doing wrong:** <!-- Luke to fill in — the CV won't tell us this, but it's the best material for opening lines -->

## Recurring Life Themes — for Opening Anecdotes

These are the kinds of situations the AI can use for scene-setting. The more specific, the better.

- **Work situations you find yourself in regularly:** Arriving at a new client site and finding the infrastructure in a state that raises questions about how it ever worked. Being the person who automates the thing everyone else has been doing manually for years without complaining about it. PCI compliance engagements where the scope grows the moment you start looking. Being brought in to fix something urgent with a fixed deadline (Ventrica: PCI compliance by December 31st — met it). Managing change control processes that exist for good reason but feel bureaucratic when you're trying to fix something obvious. Supporting people remotely — Gary in America, Dad at home — where the problem description is "it's not working" and the diagnostic process starts from zero.
- **Personal habits that connect to your work:** Building PowerShell tools during personal development time on client engagements (PSCiscoMeraki was written at Solopress; the blog itself is a product of time spent learning and tinkering). Spending time on things that probably count as work but don't feel like it — exploring local LLMs, Meshtastic, agentic development. The blog originally existed as a way to document things so Luke didn't have to remember them.
- **Things that prompted you to write a post (typical patterns):** Solving something that took longer than it should have because the answer wasn't obvious or well-documented. Building a tool or function and wanting to share it before it disappears into a private repo. A specific client problem that turned into a reusable solution. Something that worked in an unexpected way that seemed worth capturing before the details faded.

---

## Personal Life & Interests (for non-work posts or humanising asides)

- **Hobbies / interests outside work:**
  - **Airsoft** — Luke's son is heavily into airsoft and runs a team; Luke helps out and has his own kit, though hasn't played often recently due to personal circumstances.
  - **Geopolitics and cyber threat intelligence monitoring** — has a dedicated dashboard setup for following world events: SOCRadar (Iran-Israel/US conflict), LiveUAMap, WorldMonitor, PizzINT (the Pentagon Pizza Index — tracks military tension via pizza delivery patterns near defence sites), and a self-hosted WORLDVIEW orbital tracking tool. This isn't casual news-reading; it's proper OSINT-adjacent monitoring.
  - **Meshtastic and CivTAK** — off-grid mesh radio networking using LoRa hardware, combined with CivTAK (the civilian version of the American DoD situational awareness system). Running a dedicated site at [digitaltak.lukeleigh.com](https://digitaltak.lukeleigh.com/). Exploring as a potential new business venture.
  - **Local LLM exploration** — running models locally with LM Studio and Ollama, experimenting with AI coding agents including OpenCode. Actively interested in what's possible without cloud dependency.
  - **Casual gaming and streaming content** — watches gaming/streaming content for entertainment, no particular favourites.
  - **Workshop and DIY** — has a workshop; connected to Alison's resin business and general making/tinkering.

- **People — family and close friends:**
  - **Alison** — partner. Runs ARC Resin Shop from home: resin art and craft products, sold at local trade events and online. Luke provides IT and technical support for the business.
  - **Son** — heavily into airsoft; runs a team that Luke helps support. The airsoft interest is his son's first, Luke's second.
  - **Dad** — has his own house; Luke provides ongoing tech support and setup.
  - **Gary** — close friend who moved to America. Luke still provides regular remote tech support and they talk constantly, like he's still just next door.
  - **Rob Green** ([trossr32](https://github.com/trossr32)) — close friend and senior software engineer/architect; owner of Rob Green Engineering Ltd, also based in Essex. Has collaborated on and contributed to Luke's projects over the years. Fellow PowerShell enthusiast and Servarr user. Worth naming if a post references external help or collaboration.

- **Anything else the AI should know is real:**
  - The Servarr stack (Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr etc.) is a genuine part of the home setup — Rob Green has even built browser extensions for it
  - Luke and Rob have a genuine shared history on GitHub — not just online acquaintances

---

## Things the AI Should NOT Invent

The AI must not fabricate personal details that aren't in this file. If a section above is blank,
the AI should either ask Luke to fill it in, or write a generic-but-honest opening that doesn't
make up specifics.

Do not invent:
- Home lab details not confirmed here
- Family members or personal relationships not mentioned here
- Specific client names, locations, or incident details
- Opinions on products or vendors Luke hasn't expressed

---

## Voice Reminders (Quick Reference)

- Anecdotes meander before arriving at the point — that's intentional, not padding
- Admit the embarrassing thing before explaining the smart solution
- "I was one of those admins for an embarrassingly long time" is a good sentence
- The reader is a peer, not a student — write for someone who knows what they're doing but might have missed this particular thing
